title.zh: 大腦的「強迫循環」：OFC神經活動揭開強迫症瞬間動態之謎
title.en: The Brain’s Obsessive Loop: How Gamma Waves in the Orbitofrontal Cortex Drive OCD Symptoms

summary.zh: 強迫症患者總在反覆掙扎：為什麼某些時刻症狀會突然爆發？最新《Cell》研究揭開大腦深層機制。研究團隊對強迫症患者進行顱內神經監測，發現眶額皮層（OFC）的低頻伽馬波活動在症狀惡化時明顯升高，就像神經回路突然堵車。更驚人的是，當醫師刺激基底核特定區域時，症狀減輕的同時，OFC的伽馬波活動也同步下降。這項發現不只解釋了強迫症為何像「情緒過山車」般波動，更為腦深部電刺激療法提供了新方向——未來或許能透過精準調控神經活動，讓患者擺脫「強迫循環」的束縛。
summary.en: Why do obsessive-compulsive disorder symptoms surge unpredictably? A breakthrough study in *Cell* reveals how low-frequency gamma waves in the orbitofrontal cortex (OFC) drive symptom fluctuations. Researchers monitored neural activity in OCD patients during symptom-provocation tasks and found that elevated gamma power in the anteromedial OFC consistently correlated with high symptom states. Crucially, when electrical stimulation of the ventral basal ganglia reduced symptoms, gamma activity in the OFC also decreased. This mechanistic link suggests OCD symptoms are not static but dynamically regulated by real-time neural activity. For clinicians, this discovery opens pathways to refine deep brain stimulation protocols—potentially allowing precise modulation of gamma oscillations to suppress symptoms without broad brain interference.

keyArticle.title: Human orbitofrontal neural activity is linked to obsessive-compulsive behavioral dynamics
